metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Move the 'full test suite passes with all five sibling changes integrated' check out of this story's ACs and into a dedicated integration story or the last sibling story's ACs, so every AC in this story is satisfiable in isolation.
findings.finding: The AC 'The full test suite passes with all five sibling changes integrated' cannot be proven within this story's session — it requires all four sibling stories to be merged first, making it a cross-story gate rather than an in-session completable criterion.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'The full test suite passes with all five sibling changes integrated (this foundational story owns the integrated-suite check).'
findings.suggested_fix: Remove this AC from this story. Add an AC to the last sibling story (or a new thin integration story) that reads: 'The full test suite passes with all five sibling stories merged; `make test` exits 0.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit implementation rule (and corresponding scope file/AC) for the `idea → closed` bypass: specify which gates are skipped (completion-verifier, signature gate), whether `transition_close.py` needs a status-check exemption for `idea`, and add a test AC line for `transition <id> idea closed` succeeding with exit 0.
findings.finding: The plan prominently references an `idea → closed` 'reject/drop bypass' as a key lifecycle feature (mentioned in the Why section and listed as a CLAUDE.md documentation requirement), but no scope file or AC line specifies HOW this bypass is implemented. The current `close_ticket` path in `transition_close.py` runs completion-verification and signature gates. The plan does not identify `transition_close.py` as a scope file, does not say `idea` tickets are gate-exempt at close, and does not define the decision rule for which gates (if any) are bypassed on `idea → closed`.
findings.location: Plan 'What' section: 'the `idea → closed` reject/drop bypass' / Plan 'Scope' section (no mention of `transition_close.py`) / AC: CLAUDE.md documents 'close-bypass'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm that `idea`-status tickets are protected from the reconciler's `preflight_status_mapping` abort (e.g. add `idea` to `outbound_differ`'s `excluded_statuses` default, or add it to `config.local_to_jira_status`, or add `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/config.py` to Scope with an explicit interim-protection decision) — the current plan defers Jira mapping as documentation but does not prevent `StatusMappingError` from aborting reconciler passes.
findings.finding: The plan acknowledges a 'Jira IDEA mapping prerequisite' in the CLAUDE.md AC but does not address how the reconciler behaves for `idea`-status tickets in the interim — `preflight_status_mapping` in reconcile.py raises `StatusMappingError` (aborting the entire reconciler pass) for any outbound mutation referencing a status absent from `local_to_jira_status`, and `outbound_differ`'s `excluded_statuses` defaults only to `{archived, deleted}`, not `idea`.
findings.location: ## Scope (missing: src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/config.py) / ## Acceptance Criteria — 'Jira IDEA mapping prerequisite'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/config.py` to Scope with the action: add `'idea'` to the `excluded_statuses` default in `outbound_differ.OutboundDiffConfig` (or add it to `local_to_jira_status` mapped to a safe Jira status as a placeholder) so that `idea`-status tickets are skipped by the outbound differ and cannot trip `preflight_status_mapping` before the full Jira IDEA workflow is in place.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Decide whether `idea` tickets should be excluded from `validate` health checks (e.g., by adding `idea` to the `normalize_issues` status-exclusion set) and add `src/rebar/_engine_support/validate.py` to the Scope if a code change is needed, or clarify the AC to confirm the current pass-through behavior is intentional.
findings.finding: `validate.py::normalize_issues()` excludes only `error`, `fsck_needed`, and `closed` statuses before running the 9 health checks — an `idea` ticket will flow through to checks like `check_orphaned_tasks` (which treats any non-`closed` ticket as active), `check_missing_descriptions`, and `check_in_progress_without_notes`. This file is an unenumerated consumer of the `ticket_status` value set, yet the plan's AC mentions '`validate` exemptions' only as CLAUDE.md documentation.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the `transition.py` Scope entry to also update the `_USAGE` string (line 36) and the `_validate_status` error message (line 166) to include `idea` alongside `open`, `in_progress`, `closed`, and `blocked`.
findings.finding: The plan's Scope entry for `transition.py` names only `_VALID_STATUSES`, but the same file contains two additional hardcoded status enumerations — the `_USAGE` string (line 36: `open | in_progress | closed | blocked`) and the `_validate_status` error message (line 166: `Must be one of: open, in_progress, closed, blocked`) — that will become stale/incorrect when `idea` is added to `_VALID_STATUSES`. These are not mentioned in the plan's Scope or ACs.
findings.location: Scope section — `src/rebar/_commands/transition.py`; also transition.py lines 36 and 166
findings.suggested_fix: In `_USAGE` change `open | in_progress | closed | blocked` to `open | in_progress | closed | blocked | idea`. In `_validate_status` change `'Must be one of: open, in_progress, closed, blocked'` to `'Must be one of: open, in_progress, closed, blocked, idea'` (or dynamically derive it from `_VALID_STATUSES`).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `transition.py`'s `_USAGE` string (line 36) and the `_validate_status()` error message (line 167) to the Scope as edit targets alongside `_VALID_STATUSES`.
findings.finding: The `_USAGE` string in `transition.py` (line 36) hardcodes valid statuses as `open | in_progress | closed | blocked` — this string is not a generated artifact and is not listed as an edit target in the plan's Scope, so it will become stale after `idea` is added to `_VALID_STATUSES`.
findings.location: Scope section / src/rebar/_commands/transition.py:36 (_USAGE string) and line 167 (_validate_status error message)
findings.suggested_fix: In the Scope bullet for `transition.py`, expand to: '`_VALID_STATUSES`, the `_USAGE` help string, and the `_validate_status()` error message — all three hardcode the status list.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update the hardcoded status list in the `_validate_status` error message (`transition.py:166`) to include `idea`, or derive it programmatically from `_VALID_STATUSES`.
findings.finding: The `_validate_status` function in `transition.py` contains a hardcoded human-readable error string at line 166 (`'Must be one of: open, in_progress, closed, blocked'`) that is not derived from `_VALID_STATUSES`. After adding `idea` to `_VALID_STATUSES`, this string will still list only the original four statuses, silently misleading users who attempt a bad transition.
findings.suggested_fix: Change the raise to: `raise CommandError(f"Error: invalid {label} '{value}'. Must be one of: {', '.join(_VALID_STATUSES)}", returncode=1)` so the error text stays in sync with the tuple automatically.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Decide and implement whether `idea`-status tickets should be excluded from `normalize_issues` (like `closed`) or individually exempted in the relevant validate checks (e.g., `check_orphaned_tasks`), and add `src/rebar/_engine_support/validate.py` / `validate_checks.py` to the plan's scope if code changes are needed.
findings.finding: The `validate` command's `normalize_issues` function (validate.py:142) only drops tickets with status `'error'`, `'fsck_needed'`, or `'closed'` — so `idea`-status tickets will flow through all nine health checks (orphaned tasks, missing descriptions, in_progress without notes, etc.) and may generate spurious validate warnings. The plan mentions 'validate exemptions' in the CLAUDE.md documentation AC but does not address whether `idea` tickets need filtering in `normalize_issues` or an individual check (e.g., `check_orphaned_tasks` flags parentless non-epic/non-bug open tickets).
findings.location: src/rebar/_engine_support/validate.py:142 / Plan §What: 'validate exemptions' mentioned in CLAUDE.md AC; Plan §Scope does not list validate.py

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly document in CLAUDE.md (and/or in the plan's §What) whether `idea → in_progress` transitions should or should not trigger the parent-first cascade, since the current `_cascade_open_parent` guard (`current_status == 'open'`) means they will not.
findings.finding: The parent-first cascade in `_cascade_open_parent` (transition.py:323) only fires when `current_status == 'open'`. An `idea → in_progress` transition will NOT cascade a parent to `in_progress` first, unlike `open → in_progress`. The plan does not acknowledge this behavioral difference, leaving it unclear whether the `idea` status should or should not participate in the parent cascade.
findings.location: src/rebar/_commands/transition.py:323 / Plan §What: 'Add idea to the transition validator so transitions into and out of idea are legal'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (and test) for `rebar claim <idea-ticket-id>` verifying it exits non-zero with a meaningful error, confirming structural unclaimability is regression-tested.
findings.finding: The plan names structural unclaimability as a key design rationale for `idea`-status tickets, but no AC or test covers the non-happy-path where a user explicitly runs `claim <id>` on an idea-status ticket — the expected error message/exit code is unspecified and untested.
findings.suggested_fix: Add AC: `rebar claim <id>` where `<id>` has status `idea` exits non-zero (e.g., exit 1) with an error message such as 'ticket is not open'; add a unit test in the claim path asserting this behavior.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC: `rebar claim <id>` (or the equivalent claim primitive) on an `idea`-status ticket must fail with a non-zero exit / explicit error (test-verified), exercising the structural unclaimability guarantee that is the stated design rationale.
findings.finding: The plan's entire design rationale is that `idea`-status tickets are structurally unclaimable because `claim_core` only accepts `open`-status tickets — yet no AC verifies that `claim <id>` on an `idea`-status ticket is rejected. This is a new user-facing behavioral guarantee (the 'no claim genesis window' property) with zero test coverage.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (full list) / 'Why' section explaining structural unclaimability
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC: '`claim <id>` where `<id>` has status `idea` returns exit non-zero / error (test-verified).' This can be a single CLI integration test: create a ticket, transition it to `idea`, assert `claim <id>` fails.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in the plan (or scope the CLAUDE.md AC narrowly to only what IS designed in this story) exactly which `validate` rules are waived for `idea`-status tickets, or explicitly defer the validate-exemptions documentation to the child story that designs it.
findings.finding: The CLAUDE.md AC requires documenting 'validate exemptions' for `idea`-status tickets, but neither this plan nor the referenced child stories define what those exemptions are, leaving the executor without the content needed to write the documentation.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'The committed `CLAUDE.md` documents the `idea` status and its lifecycle (transitions, close-bypass, validate exemptions, Jira prerequisite).'
findings.suggested_fix: Narrow the CLAUDE.md AC to: 'The committed `CLAUDE.md` documents the `idea` status, its legal transitions, and the Jira `IDEA` mapping prerequisite; validate-exemption and close-bypass documentation is deferred to the child stories that implement those behaviors.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out plan (or confirm no exhaustive TicketStatus consumers exist) to address the risk that adding `idea` to the enum breaks existing match/switch code paths without a default handler.
findings.finding: The plan adds a new value to the `ticket_status` enum (a data shape change) but provides no rollback path or expand-contract sequence if the new value causes unexpected behavior in existing exhaustive consumers (e.g., match/switch statements on TicketStatus without a default case).
findings.suggested_fix: Add a Scope entry: 'Grep codebase for exhaustive TicketStatus consumers (match/case, if-elif chains) and add a default/wildcard branch before merging.' Also add a one-line rollback note: 'If integration breaks, revert common.schema.json and regenerate types.py — no data migration needed since idea tickets cannot yet be created.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify whether `idea`-status tickets are exempt from the plan-review gate on `-> in_progress` transitions, and if so, add `idea`'s ticket-type or status to the gate-exemption rule in `transition.py`/`gates.py` (or document that the AC uses `--force` and why).
findings.finding: The plan's AC requires `transition <id> idea in_progress` to succeed with exit 0, but `transition.py` fires the plan-review gate on ANY transition whose `target_status == "in_progress"` (line 256: `if cascade and target_status == "in_progress"`). `idea`-status tickets are not listed alongside `bug`/`session_log` as gate-exempt ticket types. The plan neither adds `idea` to the gate-exempt set, nor specifies that the AC uses `--force`, nor defines whether `idea → in_progress` triggers the plan-review gate. This decision rule is load-bearing for the AC but is entirely absent from the plan.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria: `transition <id> idea in_progress` succeeds with exit 0 / `src/rebar/_commands/transition.py` line 256

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
